第二間大行講fb
Investment Case: Primary Beneficiary of the SMB Rush to Get Online
Facebook reported revenue and Adjusted EPS of $18.7 billion and $2.37 versus CS $17.2
billion and $1.78 and consensus $17.1 billion and $1.59 respectively. There was no specific
revenue guidance given limited visibility, but management did disclose that ad revenue growth
rate remained stable at 10% YOY through the first three weeks of July. Our FY20 estimates for
revenue, Adjusted EBITDA, and Adjusted EPS are now $80.7 billion, $40.7 billion, and $10.31
respectively versus prior $78.5 billion, $37.9 billion, and $9.52. We maintain our Outperform
rating and our target rises to $315 from our prior $305.
Consistent with the prior quarter’s results, the rise in engagement afforded Facebook the
opportunity to increase ad impressions by +40% to offset ad price compression of -21%
(versus 1Q20’s +39% and -16% respectively). Businesses’ more rapid push to get online –
and particular for the SMBs – helped to deindex revenue contribution from the top 100
advertisers from 20% to 16%. We believe the most important consideration is that total
businesses using Facebook rose to 180 million from 140 million last quarter and advertisers
reached 9 million. As the resulting free tools to paying advertiser conversion rate currently
stands at ~2%, the opportunity remains for Facebook to continue to propel ad revenue growth
with a return to historical conversion rates of 5-7% with easy-to-use advertising products. With
operating expense guide range also moderating to $52 billion to $55 billion (versus prior $52
billion to $56 billion), our estimates increase once more for 2020 – as a result our price target
increases to $315 from $305 and we maintain our Outperform rating on the following thesis
points:
 Potential better-than-expected ad revenue growth given ongoing product innovation
around all of its currently-monetized properties (Instagram Checkout, Search in
Marketplaces, etc.).
 Street models are too conservative and underestimate the long-term monetization
potential of other billion-user properties like Messenger and WhatsApp.
 Optionality for faster-than-expected free cash flow growth as Facebook begins to find
greater efficiency in content screening and security.

E-commerce Efforts Taking Shape Across Shops, Pay, & Messaging. With the recent launch of FB Shops, expansion of WhatsApp Pay in India and Brazil, and ongoing work toward messaging interoperability, Facebook's e-commerce strategy continues to take shape. Shops help build awareness for SMBs and move them down the funnel, while Pay reduces friction & creates a more seamless checkout process. Messaging also enables SMBs to communicate directly w/customers and provide support. While still early, the company stated it is already scaling FB Shops quickly as brick & mortar businesses create digital storefronts seamlessly. Meanwhile, FB is working to expand its payment options through FB Pay, WhatsApp Pay (likely longer-term due to regulatory hurdles), and Checkout. FB also emphasized the importance of messaging commerce & click to messaging ads, the latter of which have exceeded expectations. Messaging commerce is especially important in developing countries, where SMBs conduct transactions over Messenger and WhatsApp, & it also helps to improve customer service. We note that WhatsApp Business now has 50M+ users, and there are now 180M+ businesses that use FB’s tools every month vs. 160M in May. While there are 5% fees tied to transactions through Checkout, FB indicated that bigger monetization impact over time would come from incremental ad dollars as FB drives more conversions. At 180M biz using FB tools & 9M advertisers, 5% penetration suggests considerable room ahead.

Investing.com – 摩根大通 (NYSE:JPM)認為，如果經濟數據不及預期，未來幾周股市可能會有適度的回調。

約翰·諾曼德等策略師在周五的壹份報告中稱，鑒於需求被壓抑，且更多的財政刺激計劃最終將通過國會，令經濟維持於“趨勢之上”，因此股市大幅下跌的可能性不大。

不過，這些策略師警告稱，月初的非農就業報告和零售銷售數據存在不及預期的適度風險。

“美國經濟增長放緩與失業救濟到期保留了8月份市場回調的風險，但鑒於投資者的倉位，其跌幅應該受限。”

目前，標普500指數已從3月份的低點上漲了46%。隨著基準股指的反彈，許多市場觀察家警告說，可能會出現回調，而且企業內部人士已經開始拋售股票。

諾曼德曾在3月股市觸底之後不久表示最糟糕的情況可能已經過去了，他認為目前並沒有出現大幅回調的條件。

摩根大通策略師說：“當大多數對沖基金以及平衡型債券/股票共同基金的股票頭寸指標低於平均水平時，很難會出現大幅度（約10%）的回調。”
